text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English, the podcast where we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听《Real Easy English》，这是一档用简单英语进行真实对话、帮助大家学习英语的播客节目。

text: I'm Neil and with me is Georgie.
zh: 我是尼尔，和我一起的是乔吉。

text: Hello!
zh: 你好！

text: Did you know that you can now watch a video of this podcast and you can read along with a transcript on our website?
zh: 你知道吗？现在你可以在我们的网站上观看这期播客的视频，还可以边看文字稿边听。

text: Visit bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 请访问 bbclearningenglish.com。

text: Hi Georgie, how are you?
zh: 嗨，乔吉，你好吗？

text: I'm very well, thank you, Neil.
zh: 我很好，谢谢，尼尔。

text: How are you?
zh: 你呢？

text: How was your journey into work?
zh: 你上班路上还顺利吗？

text: My journey into work was very good today, actually, and we're actually talking about transport today.
zh: 其实我今天上班路上挺顺利的，而且我们今天正好要聊聊交通话题。

text: How was your journey to work?
zh: 你上班路上怎么样？

text: My journey was quite easy this morning, actually.
zh: 其实我今天早上的路上挺顺利的。

text: It isn't always.
zh: 不过并不总是这样。

text: And Neil, we actually have a very similar commute, don't we?
zh: 尼尔，其实我们的通勤方式很相似，对吧？

text: I get the tube to work, which in the UK is the underground train.
zh: 我坐地铁上班，在英国叫“the underground”。

text: So I walk to the station around 15 to 20 minutes, and then I get the underground train, one train, and then I change and then get another one.
zh: 我先走大约15到20分钟去车站，然后坐地铁，坐一趟车后换乘，再坐一趟。

text: And then I walk to work.
zh: 然后再走路去上班。

text: What about you?
zh: 你呢？

text: I do that.
zh: 我也是这样。

text: But before your bit, I have to get a bus as well.
zh: 不过在你说的那部分之前，我还得先坐公交车。

text: Are you that far away from the station?
zh: 你离车站有那么远吗？

text: A couple of miles.
zh: 大概几英里吧。

text: Okay.
zh: 好的。

text: Three kilometers.
zh: 也就是三公里左右。

text: A bit too far to walk then.
zh: 那走路确实有点远。

text: A bit too far to walk.
zh: 是有点远，走不过去。

text: I have walked when the public transport has been unreliable.
zh: 公共交通不靠谱的时候，我也走过路。

text: But normally I get the bus to the tube stop and then I get on the tube into work.
zh: 但通常我是先坐公交到地铁站，然后再坐地铁去上班。

text: Lovely.
zh: 真好。

text: And do you like the tube?
zh: 那你喜欢坐地铁吗？

text: What do you think about it?
zh: 你觉得怎么样？

text: I think the tube is very convenient, actually, because the trains go very frequently.
zh: 我觉得地铁其实挺方便的，因为班次很频繁。

text: So if you miss one, you can wait just a couple of minutes and get the next one.
zh: 如果错过一班，只要等几分钟就能坐上下一班。

text: And it's probably the quickest way to get into central London because there's so much traffic.
zh: 而且这可能是进伦敦市中心最快的方式，因为路上车太多了。

text: Driving is a really bad idea.
zh: 开车真的是个很糟糕的主意。

text: Yeah, I agree with you.
zh: 是啊，我同意你的看法。

text: It is very reliable.
zh: 地铁确实很可靠。

text: it's usually very easy the thing I don't like about it especially in the morning at rush hour
zh: 通常都挺方便的，我不喜欢的一点，尤其是在早高峰的时候，

text: is the number of people on the tube um the the underground trains in London
zh: 就是地铁里的人太多了，伦敦的地铁列车

text: are very old they're very small and they are very um crowded in the mornings so crowded means there's lots of people.
zh: 很老旧，车厢也很小，早上的时候特别拥挤，“crowded”意思就是人很多。

text: We can also say packed.
zh: 我们也可以说“packed”，也是拥挤的意思。

text: Yeah, and I really don't like being in a tight space with lots of people in the mornings, so early in the morning.
zh: 是啊，我真的不喜欢一大早就和很多人挤在一个狭小的空间里。

text: Yeah, I agree.
zh: 是啊，我同意。

text: But I'm lucky because I get on the Tube at the first stop, so I always get a seat.
zh: 不过我很幸运，因为我是从第一站上车的，所以总能有座位。

text: But then by the time you get on, it is usually really packed.
zh: 但等你上车的时候，通常已经很挤了。

text: And the closer you get into central London, the more packed it is.
zh: 越靠近伦敦市中心，就越拥挤。

text: You're squashed up against strangers.
zh: 你会被挤在陌生人中间。

text: It's not always fun.
zh: 这可不总是件愉快的事。

text: I hate it.
zh: 我很讨厌这样。

text: What about buses?
zh: 那公交车呢？

text: Do you like buses?
zh: 你喜欢坐公交车吗？

text: are okay.
zh: 还可以吧。

text: In London, again, they are quite frequent and that's convenient.
zh: 在伦敦，公交车班次也挺多的，这点很方便。

text: Not always.
zh: 但也不总是这样。

text: I mean, they are.
zh: 我是说，确实挺频繁的。

text: They're frequent, but they're more unreliable than trains, aren't they?
zh: 班次是挺多，但比火车更不靠谱，对吧？

text: There always seems to be roadworks near where I live and when there are roadworks, there are traffic lights and then there are delays on the buses.
zh: 我家附近好像总有道路施工，一施工就会有交通灯管制，公交车就会因此延误。

text: So in the city, public transport can be great, but what about when we go outside of the city?
zh: 那在市内，公共交通可以说很不错，但如果我们要出城呢？

text: What do you use to go to other parts of the UK?
zh: 你会用什么方式去英国的其他地方？

text: If it's quite far, so if I'm going really far north,
zh: 如果距离比较远的话，比如说我要去很北的地方，

text: maybe to Scotland or to, I don't know, Manchester or a northern city, it's probably better to get the train because it's faster and I really like trains.
zh: 可能是苏格兰，或者比如曼彻斯特这样的北方城市，那我通常更愿意坐火车，因为更快，而且我很喜欢坐火车。

text: You can relax, it's a relaxing experience.
zh: 你可以放松，那是一种很惬意的体验。

text: Some of my best travel experiences have been on long -distance trains.
zh: 我一些最棒的旅行经历都是在长途火车上度过的。

text: Yeah?
zh: 是吗？

text: Yeah.
zh: 是啊。

text: Where have you been that's been so fantastic on a train?
zh: 你坐火车去过哪里，觉得特别棒？

text: Well, I once went from Prague to Moscow on a train.
zh: 嗯，我有一次坐火车从布拉格去莫斯科。

text: Wow.
zh: 哇。

text: Yeah.
zh: 是啊。

text: What was that like?
zh: 那感觉怎么样？

text: Really exciting.
zh: 特别刺激。

text: Why?
zh: 为什么？

text: It took about three days and I was with some friends and we could eat and drink and look out the window.
zh: 大概花了三天时间，我和几个朋友一起，我们可以边吃边喝，看着窗外的风景。

text: The landscape changed frequently.
zh: 沿途的风景一直在变化。

text: It was just exciting.
zh: 就是那种令人兴奋的感觉。

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we heard in this podcast, starting with some useful adjectives to describe public transport.
zh: 我们来回顾一下这期播客中学到的词汇，先从一些描述公共交通的实用形容词开始。

text: For example, we had crowded, which means very busy.
zh: 比如我们听到了“crowded”，意思是非常繁忙、拥挤。

text: We also hear packed.
zh: 我们也听到了“packed”，同样表示拥挤。

text: We had reliable, which describes something you can trust, and unreliable, which describes something you can't trust.
zh: 我们还听到了“reliable”，用来形容可以信赖的东西，还有“unreliable”，形容不可靠的东西。

text: We heard frequent, which describes something that happens often.
zh: 我们听到了“frequent”，用来形容经常发生的事情。

text: And we also heard delayed, which means something like public transport comes later than expected.
zh: 我们还听到了“delayed”，意思是公共交通比预期时间晚到。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 这一期《Real Easy English》就到这里。

text: Don't forget to go to our website where you can get a free worksheet to download to test what you've learned.
zh: 别忘了访问我们的网站，你可以免费下载一份练习题，来测试一下你学到的内容。

text: It's at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 网址是 bbclearningenglish.com。

text: See you then.
zh: 我们下次再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。

text: Bye.
zh: 拜拜。
